json_schema.description:
Train an RF-DETR object detection model on a Hafnia dataset.

Loads the selected managed Hafnia training dataset when running on the platform; when
executed locally, the small public sample dataset is used. (The evaluation benchmark is a
separate held-out dataset and is never loaded here.) Initializes an RF-DETR model from
the compressed model archive pointed to by ``model_path`` (optionally with pretrained weights), converts the
train/val splits to COCO format and runs RF-DETR training.

After training, every ``checkpoint_*.pth`` produced by RF-DETR is repackaged as a
standalone compressed Hafnia model archive (weights + serialized model config bundled into a
single ``.zip``) under the experiment model and checkpoints folders. The checkpoint selected
by ``inference_model_name`` is then
loaded as a ``WrappedModel``, optimized for inference (e.g. ``torch.compile`` when enabled
via ``inference_config``) and run on the held-out test split. Predictions are written to
the experiment artifacts folder. When the test split has ground-truth annotations, detection
metrics are computed via ``metric_calculations`` and logged through ``HafniaLogger``; if no
ground truth is present the metric step is skipped with a warning.

model_path.description: Path to a compressed (zip) pretrained model used as the training starting point. Options: ['pretrained_models/RFDETRNano.zip', 'pretrained_models/RFDETRMedium.zip', 'pretrained_models/RFDETRLarge.zip', 'pretrained_models/RFDETRSegNano.zip']. Note: this is ignored when a checkpoint is available (e.g. a checkpoint selected for the experiment on the Hafnia platform) - training resumes from the checkpoint instead (and '--pretrained' is forced to True).
model_path.title: Model Path

pretrained.description: Initialize the model from pretrained weights
pretrained.title: Pretrained

grad_accumulation_steps.description: Number of gradient accumulation steps (effective batch size = batch_size * grad_accumulation_steps)
grad_accumulation_steps.title: Grad Accumulation Steps

resolution.description: Input resolution (square side in pixels). Defaults to each model's built-in value.
resolution.title: Resolution

task_name.description: Dataset task name used for training. Only required when the dataset has multiple tasks matching the model primitive.
task_name.title: Task Name

samples.description: Number of samples to use for training (omit to use all samples). Use for testing purposes.
samples.title: Samples

stop_early.description: Exit before training starts. Can be used to avoid long training times when smoke-testing the pipeline.
stop_early.title: Stop Early

inference_model_name.description: Checkpoint used for the post-training benchmark on the test split. Options: ['checkpoint_best_ema', 'checkpoint_best_regular', 'checkpoint_best_total']
inference_model_name.title: Inference Model Name

inference_config.description: Inference configuration used for the post-training benchmark

augmentation_backend.description: Augmentation backend: 'cpu' (Albumentations, always available) or 'gpu'/'auto' (needs Kornia, NOT bundled). Default 'cpu'.
augmentation_backend.title: Augmentation Backend

num_workers.description: Dataloader workers; raise to relieve the data-loading bottleneck on multi-vCPU nodes
num_workers.title: Num Workers

pseudo_label.description: Self-training: before training, run the model from --model-path as a teacher over the unlabeled TEST split, keep confident predictions as pseudo ground truth, and add those images to the training set. Fully automatic (no manual labels). The teacher inference uses --inference-config (resolution/grayworld/num_select apply). NOT used for any reported L0-L4 result; kept as an exploratory research path.
pseudo_label.title: Pseudo Label

pseudo_thresholds.description: Per-class confidence thresholds for pseudo labels as 'default=0.5,Person=0.65,...'. Class names must match the dataset task classes; 'default' applies to unlisted classes.
pseudo_thresholds.title: Pseudo Thresholds

pseudo_min_boxes.description: Skip pseudo images with fewer confident boxes than this. Sparse pseudo images are mostly teacher misses turned into false background supervision (recall poisoning).
pseudo_min_boxes.title: Pseudo Min Boxes

pseudo_max_images.description: Optional cap on pseudo-labeled images added to training (keeps epoch time bounded).
pseudo_max_images.title: Pseudo Max Images

json_schema.title: mainArgs
